info.name: Mazayada API Documentation
info.description: Mazayada mobile API (v1). Token-authenticated JSON API for the Flutter client covering auctions and the citizen dashboard. All responses use a unified envelope ({data, message, meta} on success; {message, errors} on error). Money is returned in dinars; send Accept-Language (ar|fr|en) to localize responses.

item.name: Appeals
item.description:
Citizen appeals (الطعون) against an auction result. An appeal is filed from a
specific auction the user took part in, then routed through the platform admin
and the organising entity. The client only ever sees the 3 public states
(PENDING / APPROVED / REJECTED).

request.description: Full detail for a single appeal owned by the authenticated user.

body.raw: {"subject":"اعتراض على نتيجة المزاد","reason":"لم يتم احتساب مزايدتي الأخيرة."}

request.description:
Files an appeal against the result of the given auction. Requires that the
auction has closed, is still within the appeal window, and that the user
took part with at least one valid bid; only one appeal per auction.

item.name: Auction Q&A
item.description:
Registered bidders asking written questions during the inspection window
(§4 step 4). Answers are published by staff and surfaced via the public Q&A
read endpoint.

request.description: Submit a question for staff to answer. Starts as PENDING and public.

item.name: Auction registration
item.description:
The §4 flow: buy the condition book (a prerequisite that unlocks its
download), then start paid registration (the participation deposit) via the
payment gateway. Requires a verified (KYC-complete) account.

request.description:
Creates the pending book-purchase payment and returns the gateway redirect
URL. Buying the book unlocks its download and is a prerequisite for
registering. Open `redirect_url` in a web view, then re-fetch the auction.

Creates the pending deposit payment and returns the gateway redirect URL.
The condition book must already be purchased. Open `redirect_url` in a web
view; once the gateway returns, poll `GET payments/{ref}/status` (or
re-fetch the auction) to confirm.

item.name: Auctions
item.description:
Public, read-only auction browsing. Money is returned in dinars; bidders are
shown only by their deterministic alias.

query.description: Keyword — matches the title in any locale or the asset location.
User tokens (upcoming, live, closed) or raw enum values.

request.description:
Paginated, filterable list of public auctions. The filter vocabulary is
identical to the web browse page (App\Support\AuctionFilters).

Lightweight autocomplete for the browse search box — the enveloped, dinar
mobile counterpart of the web live-search dropdown. Returns up to 8 public
auctions matching the term by title (any locale) or asset location. Requires
at least 2 characters; a shorter term returns an empty list.

The reference data the mobile browse screen needs to build its filter UI:
active categories, wilayas, and the enumerated token sets (status, type,
asset class, condition, sort). Cached-friendly and locale-aware (names come
from the localized accessor). Pass `wilaya` to also get that wilaya's communes.

Full detail for a single public auction. If the bidding clock has run out
but the auction is not yet finalised, it is closed on read (winner + final
price) so the response is canonical.

query.description: Number of bids to return (1-50, default 10).

request.description:
The most recent valid bids (alias only) plus the current price. Use this to
hydrate the bid history, or poll it as a fallback when the realtime socket
is unavailable.
A cheap, pollable snapshot of the live price and clock for an auction.
Answered, public inspection questions for an auction (asker not identified).

item.name: Authentication
item.description:
Token-based authentication for the mobile app. Mirrors the web auth flows
(OTP email verification, enumeration-safe reset, secret-question recovery) but
issues a Sanctum access + refresh token pair instead of a session. The shared
OTP / user-creation logic lives in OtpService + RegisterCitizen so web and API
stay in lock-step.

request.description:
Create a CITIZEN account and email a 6-digit verification code. Does NOT log
the user in — call verify-otp with the returned user_id to obtain tokens.

Authenticate with NIN-or-email + password. Returns a token pair, or — if the
email is unverified — `data.needs_email_verification = true` and a fresh OTP.

Step 1 — email a reset code if the NIN+email pair matches an account. The
response is always neutral (does not reveal whether the account exists).

Step 2 — verify the reset code and set a new password. All existing tokens
and sessions are revoked.
Step 1 of account recovery — return the account's security question.

Step 2 of account recovery — verify the secret answer and set a new
password. All existing tokens and sessions are revoked.
Verify the registration code and receive an access + refresh token pair.
Re-issue a fresh registration code, throttled by a 60-second cooldown.

Exchange a valid refresh token for a new access + refresh pair (rotation).
Send the REFRESH token as the bearer here, not the access token.
Return the authenticated user's profile.

Revoke the current device's token pair. Pass `all=true` to revoke every
device.

item.name: Bidding
item.description:
Placing bids. Requires a verified (KYC-complete) account and a paid
registration on the auction. Rate-limited per user per auction.

request.description:
Submit a bid in WHOLE DINARS. Business-rule failures (too low, not
registered, not live, …) return 422 with the localized reason under
`errors.amount`.

item.name: Commercial Register
item.description:
The user's Commercial Register (السجل التجاري) submission. Mirrors the web flow:
submit the register data plus two scans, an admin reviews it, and only an
APPROVED register unlocks participation in auctions flagged
`requires_commerce_register`. Independent of KYC — a user may hold either, both
or neither.

request.description:
The user's Commercial Register state, the data on file, which scans are
present, and whether a (re)submit is currently allowed. `status` is null
when nothing has been submitted yet.

formdata.description: The register issue (start) date (cannot be in the future).

request.description:
Creates or updates the user's Commercial Register (multipart) and moves it to
PENDING for review. On a resubmission the previously uploaded scans are kept
unless replaced, so a rejected user who only fixes a text field need not
re-upload. A PENDING-under-review or APPROVED record is locked (403).

Streams one of the authenticated user's own Commercial Register scans from
the private disk. Allowed types: register, tax-card. A user can never reach
another user's files.

item.name: Dashboard
item.description: The citizen's home summary and their participation history.

request.description: Stat tiles, recent won auctions and the latest notifications.

query.description: One of active, won, lost, upcoming (default active).

request.description: The user's participations grouped by tab (active | won | lost | upcoming).

item.name: Documents
item.description:
The signed-in user's personal document library — every generated PDF tied to an
auction they engaged with (condition book / award / receipt / delivery report),
plus the policy-gated binary download. Streams from the private disk; access is
gated by DocumentPolicy@download.

request.description: Streams the document binary (application/pdf).

query.description: Match auction title or document name.
Document types: CONDITION_BOOK, AWARD, PAYMENT_RECEIPT, DELIVERY_REPORT.
Date preset: today, 7d, 30d, this_month, this_year, all.
One of recent, oldest, auction (default recent).

request.description:
The user's documents with the same advanced filters as the web library.
Every returned document is one the user may download.
Per-type counts and total size — the stat tiles for the mobile library.

item.name: Authenticate the request for channel access.

item.name: Financial Reports
item.description: The citizen's personal financial report (تقاريري المالية).

request.description: Aggregated KPIs, dimensions, and monthly series for the authenticated user's payments.
Paginated list of the user's individual payments, filterable by the same parameters.

item.name: KYC
item.description:
Identity verification: upload documents to the private disk, submit the profile
for review, and stream back the user's own documents. Mirrors the web KYC flow.

request.description:
The user's KYC state, which documents are on file, and whether a (re)submit
is currently allowed.

Stores a KYC document (JPG/PNG) on the private disk. Allowed types:
id-front, id-back, selfie-with-id, photo-biometric.

Validates the profile, requires all three identity documents, and moves the
account to UNDER_REVIEW.

Streams one of the authenticated user's own KYC documents from the private
disk (a user can never reach another user's files).
Paginated, newest first. The unread total is in meta.unread_count.

item.name: Payments
item.description:
The winner's final payment plus the gateway return handling and a status
endpoint the app polls after the gateway web view closes.

request.description:
Gateway return URL. Confirms (or fails) the payment set for the reference
and reports the resulting status. Idempotent — safe to call more than once.

Returns the status of every payment row sharing a gateway reference for the
authenticated user. Use this to poll after the gateway web view returns.

The winner's Decree 97-33 fee breakdown BEFORE paying: itemised fee lines
(localized labels + dinars), the deposit already credited, the net amount
still due, and the payment deadline. Read-only — nothing is charged. Lets the
mobile app show the full cost sheet before it sends the winner to the gateway.

Begins the winner's final payment (§4 step 7) and returns the gateway
redirect URL.

item.name: Profile
item.description: View and update the authenticated citizen's editable profile fields.

request.description:
Updates contact details, address and the security question. Identity fields
(NIN, names) are not editable here.

item.name: System
item.description:
Lightweight, unauthenticated endpoints to verify the API pipeline (routing,
envelope, locale, rate limiting) is wired correctly.

request.description:
Returns a simple liveness payload wrapped in the standard envelope. Useful
for smoke-testing the API plumbing and the active locale.
